The provided sources do not contain the specific hadith describing shirk as more hidden than the creeping of an ant, nor do they include the prescribed prophetic supplication for seeking refuge from known and unknown polytheism. 

Please note that the following information is not from your sources and you may want to independently verify it. 

Outside of the provided texts, classical Hadith literature records the Prophet Muhammad warning his companions that shirk (polytheism or hidden ostentation) is more stealthy and hidden than the creeping of an ant. To provide a remedy for this invisible threat, he taught a specific supplication to protect one's intentions: *"O Allah, I seek refuge in You from knowingly associating anything with You, and I ask Your forgiveness for what I do unknowingly."* This framework ensures that believers remain constantly vigilant about their sincerity while relying on divine protection against spiritual flaws they cannot perceive.
